utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , albert . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you too . so , i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , albert is a 62-year-old male , with a past medical history significant for depression , type 2 diabetes , and kidney transplant , who is here today for emergency room follow-up .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i got a notification that you were in the emergency room , but , but what were you there for ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i , uh , i was n't really , uh , staying on top of my , uh , blood sugar readings , and i felt kinda woozy over the weekend . and i was little concerned , and my wife wanted to take me in and just have me checked out .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , and was it , in fact , high ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it was .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . did you ... were you admitted to the hospital ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and , uh , are you ... did they see a reason , as to why it was elevated ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , yeah . my mother was actually in the hospital the last week. she had a bit of a fall and had to do a hip replacement. she's feeling better now but we have been just grabbing meals at the cafeteria or picking up fast food on our way home and i just really was n't monitoring what i was eating .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that's sorry to hear . and are you feeling better now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , actually , when we got home from the , uh , f- from the visit , i felt a lot better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and since then , have you been following your diet pretty closely ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . 'cause we do n't wan na end up in the hospitaltoo

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . um , okay . and , so , before that happened , how are you doing with your diet ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , during the week , i've been fine , 'cause i've been very busy . on the weekends , doing things . you're seeing people . you're having people over . it's , i- not , not as consistent on the weekend .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , is there a way that you think that that can improve ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , s- stop eating .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , let's talk about your , your kidney transplant . how are-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . you doing ? you're taking immunotherapy meds ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i've , i've been pretty diligent about it , following doctor's orders , so it's been , it's been pretty good so far .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , and y- the last time i saw that you saw dr. reyes , was about three weeks ago , and everything seemed to be fine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your kidney function is good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and in terms of your depression , how are you , how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and it's been about a , a tough , ugh , year-and-a-half or so , but i've been pretty good with it . i , i have my moments , but i- as long as i find some time to relax , at least in the afternoon , then , then it seems to work out okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , i know that we've kind of talked about holding off on medical therapy-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . 'cause you're on so many other meds .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , is that something that you wan na revisit , or do you wan na look into therapy , or do you think anything's needed right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i think i probably wan na shy away from any therapy . my , my wife got me into meditation recently and , and , uh , i , i find that relaxing . so , i think i'd like to continue that , at least for a couple more months and see how it goes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . that sounds good . all right . well , i know the nurse did a quick review of systems with you , when you-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . checked in . do you have any symptoms , any chest pain or shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: none whatsoever .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lightheadedness ? dizziness ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no ? okay . um , and i just wan na go ahead and do a quick physical exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . show me the vital signs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , looking here right now , your vital signs look great . you know , your pulse ox is great . your h- your blood pressure and heart rate are right where they should be .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , i'm gon na just check you out , and i'm gon na let you know what i find . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , on your physical exam , everything looks really good . um , you do n't appear in any distress at this time . i do n't appreciate any carotid bruits . your heart , on your heart exam , i do hear that slight 2/6 systolic ejection murmur , but we heard that in the past .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your lungs sound nice and clear , but i notice , you know , 1+ , uh , edema in your lower extremities . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so , let's go ahead . i wan na look at some of your results . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . show me the glucose .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , right now , your blood sugar is about 162 . have you eaten before you came in here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did not .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , hey , dragon . show me the diabetes labs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i'm looking at your diabetes labs . you know , your hemoglobin a1c is about 8 , and that's a , that's a little high .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , not only , you know , have your blood sugars , were they high that one day , they were , they've been a little elevated .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , we'll talk about , you know , how to go ahead and , and fix that . okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let me talk a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , for your first problem , this hyperglycemia , you know , i wan na go ahead and increase your lantus to 20 units at night . okay ? i want you to continue your monitor your blood sugar and let me know how they're running 'cause we might have to adjust that further .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? um , and i wan na order another hemoglobin a1c in a couple months . hey , dragon . order a hemoglobin a1c .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your next problem , your depression , i think you're doing a great job with your current strategies with the meditation . we will hold off on medication or therapy at this time , and you know to call me if you need anything , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and for your third problem , your kidney transplant , your kidney function looks stable . uh , i'm gon na just have you go back to dr. reyes , to manage all of your immunosuppression medications .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and then , he knows to reach out to me if he needs anything . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well , the nurse will be in soon to check you out . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: perfect .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 62-year-old male with a history of depression, type 2 diabetes, and kidney transplant, presenting for an ER follow-up.
The doctor asks the patient for the reason for his emergency room visit.
The patient reports he was not monitoring his blood sugar and felt woozy, leading his wife to take him to the ER.
The doctor asks if the patient's blood sugar was actually high.
The patient confirms his blood sugar was elevated.
The doctor asks if the patient was admitted to the hospital.
The patient was not admitted to the hospital.
The doctor asks if the reason for the elevated blood sugar was identified.
The patient attributes the elevated blood sugar to poor diet and lack of monitoring while caring for his mother in the hospital.
The doctor asks if the patient is feeling better now.
The patient reports feeling better after returning home.
The doctor asks if the patient has been following his diet closely since the ER visit.
The patient confirms he has been following his diet closely.
The doctor asks how the patient was managing his diet before the ER visit.
The patient reports his diet was consistent during the week but inconsistent on weekends.
The doctor asks if the patient has ideas for improving his diet.
Kidney transplant
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his immunotherapy medications.
The patient reports being diligent with his immunotherapy medications and following doctor's orders.
The doctor notes the patient saw Dr. Reyes three weeks ago and everything was fine.
The doctor confirms the patient's kidney function is good.
Depression
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with his depression.
The patient reports he has been doing well with his depression for the past year and a half by finding time to relax.
The doctor asks if the patient wants to revisit medical therapy or look into counseling for his depression.
The patient declines therapy and prefers to continue meditation for his depression.
The doctor asks if the patient has any chest pain or shortness of breath.
The patient denies chest pain or shortness of breath.
The doctor asks if the patient has any lightheadedness or dizziness.
The patient denies lightheadedness or dizziness.
The patient's vital signs, including pulse ox, blood pressure, and heart rate, are normal.
Physical exam findings: no distress, no carotid bruits, 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs.
Physical exam finding: 1+ edema in the lower extremities.
The patient's fasting blood glucose is 162.
The doctor asks if the patient ate before the appointment.
The patient confirms he did not eat before the appointment.
The patient's hemoglobin A1c is 8, which is elevated.
The doctor increases Lantus insulin to 20 units at night and instructs the patient to continue monitoring blood sugar.
The doctor orders a repeat hemoglobin A1c in a couple of months.
The doctor advises continuing meditation for depression and holding off on medication or therapy.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue seeing Dr. Reyes for management of immunosuppression medications.
